_comment: Copy this file to config.json and fill in your ids. Everything the bot does is controlled from here - no code changes needed. See docs/GUIDE.md for a full explanation of every field.
_comment_audiences: Who is EXPECTED at events. Any mix of: role_ids (recommended), user_ids (explicit list), channel_access (everyone who can see that channel - the workaround when channels don't map to roles).
_comment_rules: Which audience each event gets. First match wins; no match = default. Two teams x two raids a week = point each team's signup channel (or title keyword) at its audience.
_comment_windows: Hours-before-start at which sign-up reminders fire. [240] = 10 days, which covers a full lockout week posted in advance: by Friday's digest the following Sunday is already ~220h out, so a narrower window silently drops it.
_comment_horizon: How far ahead the bot LOOKS for events. Does NOT change when reminders send - that is reminder_windows_hours above. 336 = 14 days, so recurring events posted a week or more out (Raid-Helper does this) show up in the log instead of looking like the bot is broken.
_comment_send_hour: Hour (0-23, US Eastern) the Friday reminder DMs are allowed to send. remind.yml fires at BOTH 21:00 and 22:00 UTC because GitHub cron cannot follow DST; this pins the actual send to 5PM Eastern year-round by making the wrong one a no-op. Set to null to let any scheduled run send.
_comment_summary_hour: Hour (0-23, US Eastern) the Sunday officer list is allowed to post. summary.yml fires at BOTH 14:00 and 15:00 UTC for the same DST reason. This run sends NO DMs and writes NO state - it only posts the still-unsigned roll-up to discord.log_channel_id. Set to null to let any scheduled run post.
_comment_digest: true = members missing from SEVERAL events get ONE combined DM listing them all, instead of one DM per event.

messages.default: Hey {member_name}! You haven't signed up for **{event_title}** yet. It starts {event_time} ({event_time_relative}). Sign up here: {signup_link}
messages.digest_header: Hey {member_name}! You haven't signed up for these upcoming raids yet:
messages.fallback: (couldn't DM you) You have upcoming raids you haven't signed up for - please check the signup channels!

_comment_announcements: Channel messages posted N minutes before each event starts, in the event's own channel (or channel_id override). Optional match scopes an announcement to certain events. mention_audience=true pings the team that owns the raid, reusing the audience_rules above so there is no second mapping to maintain; mention_role_ids is the fallback for when an audience carries no roles.

announcements.text: Raid invites has started for tonight's raid. Please have your gear and consumes. Whisper Kcin or an Officer for an invite!

_comment_no_response: Sign-up types that should STILL be nagged. Empty = anyone who clicked anything (Bench/Late/Tentative/Absence) is left alone.
